sentences.text: Mă numesc Hastings
sentences.original: So far, in the cases which I have recorded, Poirot’s investigations have started from the central fact, whether murder or robbery, and have proceeded from thence by a process of logical deduction to the final triumphant unravelling.

sentences.text: și locuiesc la Londra
sentences.original: In the events I am now about to chronicle, a remarkable chain of circumstances led from the apparently trivial incidents which first attracted Poirot’s attention to the sinister happenings which completed a most unusual case.

sentences.text: cu prietenul meu Hercule Poirot.
sentences.original: I had been spending the evening with an old friend of mine, Gerald Parker.

sentences.text: El este un detectiv belgian
sentences.original: There had been, perhaps, about half a dozen people there besides my host and myself, and the talk fell, as it was bound to do sooner or later wherever Parker found himself, on the subject of house-hunting in London.

sentences.text: și este cel mai deștept om pe care îl cunosc.
sentences.translationRu: и самый умный человек, которого я знаю.
sentences.original: Houses and flats were Parker’s special hobby. Since the end of the War, he had occupied at least half a dozen different flats and maisonnettes. No sooner was he settled anywhere than he would light unexpectedly upon a new find, and would forthwith depart bag and baggage. His moves were nearly always accomplished at a slight pecuniary gain, for he had a shrewd business head, but it was sheer love of the sport that actuated him, and not a desire to make money at it.

sentences.text: Într-o seară am fost invitat la o cină.
sentences.translationRu: Однажды вечером меня пригласили на ужин.
sentences.original: We listened to Parker for some time with the respect of the novice for the expert. Then it was our turn, and a perfect babel of tongues was let loose.

sentences.text: La masă, oamenii vorbeau
sentences.translationRu: За столом гости говорили о том,
sentences.original: Finally the floor was left to Mrs. Robinson, a charming little bride who was there with her husband.

sentences.text: despre cât de greu este
sentences.original: I had never met them before, as Robinson was only a recent acquaintance of Parker’s.  “Talking of flats,” she said,

sentences.text: să găsești un apartament bun la Londra.
sentences.original: “have you heard of our piece of luck, Mr. Parker?

sentences.text: Lângă mine stătea o doamnă tânără
sentences.translationRu: Рядом со мной сидела молодая дама
sentences.original: We’ve got a flat—at last!

sentences.text: pe nume doamna Robinson.
sentences.original: In Montagu Mansions.”

sentences.text: Avea părul roșu, ochii albaștri
sentences.translationRu: У неё были рыжие волосы, голубые глаза
sentences.original: “Well,” said Parker, “I’ve always said there are plenty of flats—at a price!”

sentences.text: și era foarte frumoasă.
sentences.original: “Yes, but this isn’t at a price.

sentences.text: Soțul meu și cu mine am avut noroc, a spus ea.
sentences.translationRu: Нам с мужем повезло, сказала она.
sentences.original: It’s dirt cheap. Eighty pounds a year!”  “But—but Montagu Mansions is just off Knightsbridge, isn’t it? Big handsome building. Or are you talking of a poor relation of the same name stuck in the slums somewhere?”  “No, it’s the Knightsbridge one. That’s what makes it so wonderful.”

sentences.text: Am găsit un apartament minunat
sentences.original: “Wonderful is the word! It’s a blinking miracle.

sentences.text: într-un cartier scump și elegant.
sentences.original: But there must be a catch somewhere. Big premium, I suppose?”  “No premium!”  “No prem—oh, hold my head, somebody!” groaned Parker.

sentences.text: Și plătim foarte puțin pentru el.
sentences.translationRu: И платим за неё совсем мало.
sentences.original: “But we’ve got to buy the furniture,” continued Mrs. Robinson.  “Ah!” Parker brisked up. “I knew there was a catch!”  “For fifty pounds. And it’s beautifully furnished!”

sentences.text: Toți au rămas surprinși
sentences.original: “I give it up,” said Parker. “The present occupants must be lunatics with a taste for philanthropy.”  Mrs. Robinson was looking a little troubled. A little pucker appeared between her dainty brows.

sentences.text: fiindcă un apartament ca acela
sentences.original: “It is queer, isn’t it?

sentences.text: costă de obicei mult mai mult.
sentences.original: You don’t think that—that—the place is haunted?”  “Never heard of a haunted flat,” declared Parker decisively.  “N-o.” Mrs. Robinson appeared far from convinced. “But there were several things about it all that struck me as—well, queer.”

sentences.text: Probabil are ceva ascuns, a râs un domn.
sentences.translationRu: Наверное, тут какой-то подвох, засмеялся один господин.
sentences.original: “For instance——” I suggested.  “Ah,” said Parker, “our criminal expert’s attention is aroused! Unburden yourself to him, Mrs. Robinson. Hastings is a great unraveller of mysteries.”  I laughed, embarrassed but not wholly displeased with the rôle thrust upon me.  “Oh, not really queer, Captain Hastings, but when we went to the agents, Stosser and Paul—we hadn’t tried them before because they only have the expensive Mayfair flats, but we thought at any rate it would do no harm—everything they offered us was four and five hundred a year, or else huge premiums, and then, just as we were going, they mentioned that they had a flat at eighty, but that they doubted if it would be any good our going there, because it had been on their books some time and they had sent so many people to see it that it was almost sure to be taken—‘snapped up’ as the clerk put it—only people were so tiresome in not letting them know, and then they went on sending, and people get annoyed at being sent to a place that had, perhaps, been let some time.”  Mrs. Robinson paused for some much needed breath, and then continued: “We thanked him, and said that we quite understood it would probably be no good, but that we should like an order all the same—just in case. And we went there straight away in a taxi, for, after all, you never know. No. 4 was on the second floor, and just as we were waiting for the lift, Elsie Ferguson—she’s a friend of mine, Captain Hastings, and they are looking for a flat too—came hurrying down the stairs. ‘Ahead of you for once, my dear,’ she said. ‘But it’s no good. It’s already let.’ That seemed to finish it, but—well, as John said, the place was very cheap, we could afford to give more, and perhaps if we offered a premium.——A horrid thing to do, of course, and I feel quite ashamed of telling you, but you know what flat-hunting is.”  I assured her that I was well aware that in the struggle for house-room the baser side of human nature frequently triumphed over the higher, and that the well-known rule of dog eat dog always applied.  “So we went up and, would you believe it, the flat wasn’t let at all. We were shown over it by the maid, and then we saw the mistress, and the thing was settled then and there. Immediate possession and fifty pounds for the furniture. We signed the agreement next day, and we are to move in to-morrow!” Mrs. Robinson paused triumphantly.  “And what about Mrs. Ferguson?” asked Parker. “Let’s have your deductions, Hastings.”  “‘Obvious, my dear Watson,’” I quoted lightly. “She went to the wrong flat.”  “Oh, Captain Hastings, how clever of you!” cried Mrs. Robinson admiringly.  I rather wished Poirot had been there. Sometimes I have the feeling that he rather underestimates my capabilities.

sentences.text: Când m-am întors acasă în acea seară,
sentences.translationRu: Когда тем вечером я вернулся домой,
sentences.original: • • • • • • •   The whole thing was rather amusing, and I propounded the thing as a mock problem to Poirot on the following morning.

sentences.text: i-am povestit lui Poirot despre apartamentul ieftin.
sentences.translationRu: я рассказал Пуаро про дешёвую квартиру.
sentences.original: He seemed interested, and questioned me rather narrowly as to the rents of flats in various localities.  “A curious story,” he said thoughtfully.

sentences.text: El a devenit imediat foarte atent.
sentences.original: “Excuse me, Hastings, I must take a short stroll.”  When he returned, about an hour later, his eyes were gleaming with a peculiar excitement. He laid his stick on the table, and brushed the nap of his hat with his usual tender care before he spoke.

sentences.text: Asta este foarte ciudat, prietene, a spus el.
sentences.translationRu: Это очень странно, друг мой, сказал он.
sentences.original: “It is as well, mon ami, that we have no affairs of moment on hand. We can devote ourselves wholly to the present investigation.”  “What investigation are you talking about?”  “The remarkable cheapness of your friend’s, Mrs. Robinson’s, new flat.”  “Poirot, you are not serious!”  “I am most serious.

sentences.text: Un apartament ca acesta
sentences.original: Figure to yourself, my friend, that the real rent of those flats is £350. I have just ascertained that from the landlord’s agents.

sentences.text: ar trebui să coste de patru ori mai mult.
sentences.translationRu: должна стоить в четыре раза дороже.
sentences.original: And yet this particular flat is being sublet at eighty pounds! Why?”

sentences.text: În viață, norocul prea mare este suspect.
sentences.translationRu: В жизни слишком большая удача подозрительна.
sentences.original: “There must be something wrong with it. Perhaps it is haunted, as Mrs. Robinson suggested.”  Poirot shook his head in a dissatisfied manner.  “Then again how curious it is that her friend tells her the flat is let, and, when she goes up, behold, it is not so at all!”  “But surely you agree with me that the other woman must have gone to the wrong flat. That is the only possible solution.”  “You may or may not be right on that point, Hastings. The fact still remains that numerous other applicants were sent to see it, and yet, in spite of its remarkable cheapness, it was still in the market when Mrs. Robinson arrived.”  “That shows that there must be something wrong about it.”  “Mrs. Robinson did not seem to notice anything amiss. Very curious, is it not? Did she impress you as being a truthful woman, Hastings?”  “She was a delightful creature!”  “Évidemment! since she renders you incapable of replying to my question. Describe her to me, then.”  “Well, she’s tall and fair; her hair’s really a beautiful shade of auburn——”  “Always you have had a penchant for auburn hair!” murmured Poirot. “But continue.”  “Blue eyes and a very nice complexion and—well, that’s all, I think,” I concluded lamely.  “And her husband?”  “Oh, he’s quite a nice fellow—nothing startling.”  “Dark or fair?”  “I don’t know—betwixt and between, and just an ordinary sort of face.”  Poirot nodded.  “Yes, there are hundreds of these average men—and, anyway, you bring more sympathy and appreciation to your description of women. Do you know anything about these people? Does Parker know them well.”  “They are just recent acquaintances, I believe. But surely, Poirot, you don’t think for an instant——”  Poirot raised his hand.  “Tout doucement, mon ami. Have I said that I think anything? All I say is—it is a curious story. And there is nothing to throw light upon it; except perhaps the lady’s name, eh, Hastings?”  “Her name is Stella,” I said stiffly, “but I don’t see——”  Poirot interrupted me with a tremendous chuckle. Something seemed to be amusing him vastly.  “And Stella means a star, does it not? Famous!”  “What on earth——”  “And stars give light! Voilà! Calm yourself, Hastings. Do not put on that air of injured dignity.

sentences.text: Mâine mergem să vedem acel apartament.
sentences.translationRu: Завтра мы пойдём посмотреть на эту квартиру.
sentences.original: Come, we will go to Montagu Mansions and make a few inquiries.”

sentences.text: A doua zi dimineață ne-am dus la clădire.
sentences.translationRu: На следующее утро мы отправились к дому.
sentences.original: I accompanied him, nothing loath. The Mansions were a handsome block of buildings in excellent repair. A uniformed porter was sunning himself on the threshold, and it was to him that Poirot addressed himself:

sentences.text: Poirot a vorbit cu portarul
sentences.original: “Pardon, but could you tell me if a Mr. and Mrs. Robinson reside here?”  The porter was a man of few words and apparently of a sour or suspicious disposition. He hardly looked at us

sentences.text: și i-a pus multe întrebări.
sentences.original: and grunted out:  “No. 4. Second floor.”  “I thank you. Can you tell me how long they have been here?”

sentences.text: Mulți oameni au vrut acel apartament, a spus portarul.
sentences.translationRu: Многие хотели снять эту квартиру, сказал швейцар.
sentences.original: “Six months.”  I started forward in amazement, conscious as I did so of Poirot’s malicious grin.  “Impossible,” I cried. “You must be making a mistake.”  “Six months.”  “Are you sure?

sentences.text: Veneau în fiecare zi să-l vadă.
sentences.translationRu: Каждый день приходили на неё посмотреть.
sentences.original: The lady I mean is tall and fair with reddish gold hair and——”  “That’s ’er,” said the porter. “Come in the Michaelmas quarter, they did. Just six months ago.”

sentences.text: Dar familia Robinson l-a primit
sentences.original: He appeared to lose interest in us and retreated slowly up the hall. I followed Poirot outside.  “Eh bien, Hastings?” my friend demanded slyly.

sentences.text: la un preț foarte mic.
sentences.original: “Are you so sure now that delightful women always speak the truth?”  I did not reply.

sentences.text: Asta nu este normal, mi-a șoptit Poirot.
sentences.original: Poirot had steered his way into Brompton Road before I asked him what he was going to do and where we were going.

sentences.text: De ce să dai un apartament ieftin
sentences.original: “To the house agents, Hastings. I have a great desire to have a flat in Montagu Mansions.

sentences.text: când atâția oameni vor să plătească mai mult?
sentences.translationRu: когда столько людей готовы платить больше?
sentences.original: If I am not mistaken, several interesting things will take place there before long.”  We were fortunate in our quest. No. 8, on the fourth floor, was to be let furnished at ten guineas a week. Poirot promptly took it for a month.

sentences.text: Cineva voia o anumită persoană în acel apartament.
sentences.translationRu: Кто-то хотел поселить в этой квартире определённого человека.
sentences.original: Outside in the street again, he silenced my protests:  “But I make money nowadays! Why should I not indulge a whim?

sentences.text: Nu înțeleg nimic, am mărturisit eu.
sentences.translationRu: Я ничего не понимаю, признался я.
sentences.original: By the way, Hastings, have you a revolver?”  “Yes—somewhere,” I answered, slightly thrilled. “Do you think——”

sentences.text: Vei înțelege în curând, a spus Poirot calm.
sentences.original: “That you will need it? It is quite possible. The idea pleases you, I see. Always the spectacular and romantic appeals to you.”

sentences.text: Trebuie doar să așteptăm puțin.
sentences.original: The following day saw us installed in our temporary home. The flat was pleasantly furnished. It occupied the same position in the building as that of the Robinsons, but was two floors higher.  The day after our installation was a Sunday.

sentences.text: Câteva zile mai târziu
sentences.original: In the afternoon, Poirot left the front door ajar, and summoned me hastily as a bang reverberated from somewhere below.

sentences.text: a venit la noi inspectorul Japp.
sentences.original: “Look over the banisters. Are those your friends. Do not let them see you.”  I craned my neck over the staircase.  “That’s them,” I declared in an ungrammatical whisper.  “Good. Wait awhile.”  About half an hour later, a young woman emerged in brilliant and varied clothing.

sentences.text: El este un vechi prieten de la poliție
sentences.original: With a sigh of satisfaction, Poirot tiptoed back into the flat.  “C’est ça. After the master and mistress, the maid. The flat should now be empty.”

sentences.text: și avea o veste importantă.
sentences.original: “What are we going to do?” I asked uneasily.

sentences.text: Avem o problemă serioasă, a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: У нас серьёзная проблема, сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: Poirot had trotted briskly into the scullery and was hauling at the rope of the coal-lift.  “We are about to descend after the method of the dustbins,” he explained cheerfully.

sentences.text: Acum câteva luni, în America,
sentences.original: “No one will observe us. The Sunday concert, the Sunday ‘afternoon out,’ and finally the Sunday nap after the Sunday dinner of England—le rosbif—all these will distract attention from the doings of Hercule Poirot. Come, my friend.”  He stepped into the rough wooden contrivance and I followed him gingerly.

sentences.text: au dispărut planurile secrete ale unui submarin nou.
sentences.translationRu: пропали секретные чертежи новой подводной лодки.
sentences.original: “Are we going to break into the flat?” I asked dubiously.  Poirot’s answer was not too reassuring:  “Not precisely to-day,” he replied.  Pulling on the rope, we descended slowly till we reached the second floor.

sentences.text: Aceste planuri sunt foarte valoroase.
sentences.original: Poirot uttered an exclamation of satisfaction as he perceived that the wooden door into the scullery was open.

sentences.text: Un italian pe nume Luigi Valdarno le-a furat, a continuat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: Их украл итальянец по имени Луиджи Вальдарно, продолжил Джепп.
sentences.original: “You observe? Never do they bolt these doors in the daytime. And yet anyone could mount or descend as we have done. At night yes—though not always then—and it is against that that we are going to make provision.”  He had drawn some tools from his pocket as he spoke, and at once set deftly to work, his object being to arrange the bolt so that it could be pulled back from the lift. The operation only occupied about three minutes. Then Poirot returned the tools to his pocket, and we reascended once more to our own domain.

sentences.text: Dar acum este mort.
sentences.original: • • • • • • •   On Monday Poirot was out all day, but when he returned in the evening he flung himself into his chair with a sigh of satisfaction.  “Hastings, shall I recount to you a little history? A story after your own heart and which will remind you of your favourite cinema?”  “Go ahead,” I laughed. “I presume that it is a true story, not one of your efforts of fancy.”  “It is true enough. Inspector Japp of Scotland Yard will vouch for its accuracy, since it was through his kind offices that it came to my ears. Listen, Hastings.

sentences.text: L-au găsit fără viață la New York.
sentences.original: A little over six months ago some important Naval plans were stolen from an American Government department.

sentences.text: Înainte să moară, a dat planurile unei femei.
sentences.translationRu: Прежде чем умереть, он отдал чертежи одной женщине.
sentences.original: They showed the position of some of the most important Harbour defences, and would be worth a considerable sum to any foreign Government—that of Japan, for example.

sentences.text: Această femeie este o spioană internațională.
sentences.original: Suspicion fell upon a young man named Luigi Valdarno, an Italian by birth, who was employed in a minor capacity in the Department and who was missing at the same time as the papers.

sentences.text: Se numește Elsa Hardt
sentences.original: Whether Luigi Valdarno was the thief or not, he was found two days later on the East Side in New York, shot dead. The papers were not on him.

sentences.text: și în public este cântăreață de operă.
sentences.translationRu: и на публике она оперная певица.
sentences.original: Now for some time past Luigi Valdarno had been going about with a Miss Elsa Hardt, a young concert singer who had recently appeared and who lived with a brother in an apartment in Washington.

sentences.text: Credem că a fugit aici, în Anglia.
sentences.translationRu: Мы думаем, что она сбежала сюда, в Англию.
sentences.original: Nothing was known of the antecedents of Miss Elsa Hardt, and she disappeared suddenly about the time of Valdarno’s death. There are reasons for believing that she was in reality an accomplished international spy who has done much nefarious work under various aliases. The American Secret Service, whilst doing their best to trace her, also kept an eye upon certain insignificant Japanese gentlemen living in Washington. They felt pretty certain that, when Elsa Hardt had covered her tracks sufficiently, she would approach the gentlemen in question. One of them left suddenly for England a fortnight ago. On the face of it, therefore, it would seem that Elsa Hardt is in England.”

sentences.text: Iată descrierea ei, a spus Japp.
sentences.original: Poirot paused, and then added softly: “The official description of Elsa Hardt is:

sentences.text: Are părul roșu, ochii albaștri
sentences.translationRu: У неё рыжие волосы, голубые глаза
sentences.original: Height 5 ft. 7, eyes blue, hair auburn,

sentences.text: și pielea deschisă la culoare.
sentences.original: fair complexion, nose straight, no special distinguishing marks.”

sentences.text: Poirot a ascultat totul cu mare interes.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро слушал всё с большим интересом.
sentences.original: “Mrs. Robinson!” I gasped.  “Well, there is a chance of it, anyhow,” amended Poirot.

sentences.text: Hastings, această descriere
sentences.original: “Also, I learn that a swarthy man, a foreigner of some kind, was inquiring about the occupants of No. 4 only this morning. Therefore, mon ami,

sentences.text: este exact ca a doamnei Robinson, a spus el.
sentences.translationRu: точь-в-точь как у миссис Робинсон, сказал он.
sentences.original: I fear that you must forswear your beauty sleep to-night, and join me in my all-night vigil in the flat below—armed with that excellent revolver of yours, bien entendu!”

sentences.text: Acum totul devine clar.
sentences.original: “Rather,” I cried with enthusiasm. “When shall we start?”  “The hour of midnight is both solemn and suitable, I fancy. Nothing is likely to occur before then.”

sentences.text: Ascultați-mă cu atenție, a spus Poirot.
sentences.original: At twelve o’clock precisely, we crept cautiously into the coal-lift and lowered ourselves to the second floor.

sentences.text: Apartamentul ieftin nu este un noroc.
sentences.original: Under Poirot’s manipulation, the wooden door quickly swung inwards, and we climbed into the flat. From the scullery we passed into the kitchen where we established ourselves comfortably in two chairs with the door into the hall ajar.

sentences.text: Este o capcană foarte inteligentă.
sentences.original: “Now we have but to wait,” said Poirot contentedly, closing his eyes.

sentences.text: Elsa Hardt și oamenii ei
sentences.original: To me, the waiting appeared endless. I was terrified of going to sleep. Just when it seemed to me that I had been there about eight hours—and had, as I found out afterwards, in reality been exactly one hour and twenty minutes—a faint scratching sound came to my ears.

sentences.text: au închiriat mai întâi acest apartament.
sentences.original: Poirot’s hand touched mine. I rose, and together we moved carefully in the direction of the hall. The noise came from there. Poirot placed his lips to my ear.

sentences.text: Apoi au plecat în secret
sentences.original: “Outside the front door. They are cutting out the lock. When I give the word, not before, fall upon him from behind and hold him fast. Be careful, he will have a knife.”  Presently there was a rending sound, and a little circle of light appeared through the door. It was extinguished immediately and then the door was slowly opened.

sentences.text: și l-au oferit foarte ieftin.
sentences.original: Poirot and I flattened ourselves against the wall.

sentences.text: Ei aveau nevoie de o doamnă Robinson, a explicat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Им нужна была некая миссис Робинсон, объяснил Пуаро.
sentences.original: I heard a man’s breathing as he passed us. Then he flashed on his torch, and as he did so, Poirot hissed in my ear:  “Allez.”

sentences.text: Robinson este un nume foarte obișnuit în Anglia.
sentences.translationRu: Робинсон — очень обычная фамилия в Англии.
sentences.original: We sprang together, Poirot with a quick movement enveloped the intruder’s head with a light woollen scarf whilst I pinioned his arms.

sentences.text: Printre miile de cupluri tinere din Londra,
sentences.translationRu: Среди тысяч молодых пар в Лондоне
sentences.original: The whole affair was quick and noiseless.

sentences.text: sigur va veni o doamnă Robinson cu părul roșu.
sentences.original: I twisted a dagger from his hand, and as Poirot brought down the scarf from his eyes, whilst keeping it wound tightly round his mouth, I jerked up my revolver where he could see it and understand that resistance was useless.

sentences.text: Și planul lor a funcționat, a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: И их план сработал, сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: As he ceased to struggle Poirot put his mouth close to his ear and began to whisper rapidly.

sentences.text: O doamnă Robinson nevinovată a luat apartamentul.
sentences.translationRu: Ни в чём не повинная миссис Робинсон сняла квартиру.
sentences.original: After a minute the man nodded.

sentences.text: Ea seamănă cu spioana Elsa Hardt.
sentences.translationRu: Она похожа на шпионку Эльзу Хардт.
sentences.original: Then enjoining silence with a movement of the hand, Poirot led the way out of the flat and down the stairs. Our captive followed, and I brought up the rear with the revolver.

sentences.text: Dar de ce era nevoie de asta? am întrebat eu.
sentences.translationRu: Но зачем это было нужно? спросил я.
sentences.original: When we were out in the street, Poirot turned to me.  “There is a taxi waiting just round the corner. Give me the revolver. We shall not need it now.”  “But if this fellow tries to escape?”  Poirot smiled.  “He will not.”

sentences.text: Pentru că niște oameni periculoși o caută pe Elsa Hardt, a răspuns Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Потому что опасные люди разыскивают Эльзу Хардт, ответил Пуаро.
sentences.original: I returned in a minute with the waiting taxi. The scarf had been unwound from the stranger’s face, and I gave a start of surprise.  “He’s not a Jap,” I ejaculated in a whisper to Poirot.  “Observation was always your strong point, Hastings! Nothing escapes you. No, the man is not a Jap. He is an Italian.”

sentences.text: Ei vor să se răzbune pentru moartea lui Valdarno.
sentences.translationRu: Они хотят отомстить за смерть Вальдарно.
sentences.original: We got into the taxi, and Poirot gave the driver an address in St. John’s Wood. I was by now completely fogged. I did not like to ask Poirot where we were going in front of our captive, and strove in vain to obtain some light upon the proceedings.

sentences.text: Acești oameni vor merge la apartament, a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Эти люди явятся в квартиру, сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: We alighted at the door of a small house standing back from the road. A returning wayfarer, slightly drunk, was lurching along the pavement and almost collided with Poirot, who said something sharply to him which I did not catch. All three of us went up the steps of the house. Poirot rang the bell and motioned us to stand a little aside. There was no answer and he rang again and then seized the knocker which he plied for some minutes vigorously.

sentences.text: Vor crede că doamna Robinson este Elsa Hardt
sentences.translationRu: Они решат, что миссис Робинсон — это Эльза Хардт,
sentences.original: A light appeared suddenly above the fanlight, and the door was opened cautiously a little way.  “What the devil do you want?” a man’s voice demanded harshly.  “I want the doctor. My wife is taken ill.”  “There’s no doctor here.”  The man prepared to shut the door, but Poirot thrust his foot in adroitly. He became suddenly a perfect caricature of an infuriated Frenchman.  “What you say, there is no doctor? I will have the law of you. You must come! I will stay here and ring and knock all night.”  “My dear sir——” The door was opened again, the man, clad in a dressing-gown and slippers, stepped forward to pacify Poirot with an uneasy glance round.  “I will call the police.”  Poirot prepared to descend the steps.  “No, don’t do that for Heaven’s sake!” The man dashed after him.  With a neat push Poirot sent him staggering down the steps. In another minute all three of us were inside the door and it was pushed to and bolted.  “Quick—in here.” Poirot led the way into the nearest room switching on the light as he did so. “And you—behind the curtain.”  “Si, signor,” said the Italian and slid rapidly behind the full folds of rose-coloured velvet which draped the embrasure of the window.

sentences.text: și o vor ataca pe femeia greșită.
sentences.translationRu: и нападут не на ту женщину.
sentences.original: Not a minute too soon. Just as he disappeared from view a woman rushed into the room.

sentences.text: Între timp, adevărata spioană va fi în siguranță.
sentences.translationRu: А тем временем настоящая шпионка будет в безопасности.
sentences.original: She was tall with reddish hair and held a scarlet kimono round her slender form.  “Where is my husband?” she cried, with a quick frightened glance. “Who are you?”

sentences.text: Este teribil! am strigat eu.
sentences.original: Poirot stepped forward with a bow.  “It is to be hoped your husband will not suffer from a chill. I observed that he had slippers on his feet, and that his dressing-gown was a warm one.”  “Who are you? What are you doing in my house?”

sentences.text: Trebuie să salvăm familia Robinson!
sentences.original: “It is true that none of us have the pleasure of your acquaintance, madame. It is especially to be regretted as one of our number has come specially from New York in order to meet you.”

sentences.text: Exact, prietene, a spus Poirot serios.
sentences.translationRu: Именно так, друг мой, серьёзно сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: The curtains parted and the Italian stepped out. To my horror I observed that he was brandishing my revolver, which Poirot must doubtless have put down through inadvertence in the cab.  The woman gave a piercing scream and turned to fly, but Poirot was standing in front of the closed door.  “Let me by,” she shrieked. “He will murder me.”  “Who was it dat croaked Luigi Valdarno?” asked the Italian hoarsely, brandishing the weapon, and sweeping each one of us with it. We dared not move.  “My God, Poirot, this is awful. What shall we do?” I cried.  “You will oblige me by refraining from talking so much, Hastings. I can assure you that our friend will not shoot until I give the word.”  “Youse sure o’ dat, eh?” said the Italian, leering unpleasantly.  It was more than I was, but the woman turned to Poirot like a flash.

sentences.text: Și avem deja un plan.
